content: To be eligible to vote in the 2026 election, you must be a citizen of the United States, be a resident of the state you are registering in, and be at least 18 years old on or before Election Day (November 3, 2026).

Photo ID requirements vary by state. Common accepted forms include a state-issued driver's license, a US passport, a military ID, or a state voter identification card. Some states allow non-photo ID like utility bills or bank statements.

Most states allow absentee or mail-in voting. Deadlines to request an absentee ballot typically fall 7-14 days before the election. Ballots must be postmarked by Election Day (November 3, 2026) to be counted.
The standard registration deadline for the General Election is October 5, 2026. However, some states allow Same Day Registration at polling locations.
